steps.title: Use the given line of best fit
steps.content: No new regression is needed because the equation of the line of best fit is already given: $y=0.096x-0.488$. The slope is the coefficient of $x$, so the slope is $0.096$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope units
steps.content: The $x$-axis is years since 1940, measured in individual years. The $y$-axis is minimum wage in dollars per hour. Therefore a slope of $0.096$ means the model predicts an increase of $0.096$ dollars per hour for each additional year.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A says the average increase was $0.096$ dollars each year. Choices C and D incorrectly treat the change as per 10 years, and choice B uses the intercept instead of the slope. Choice A is correct.
